svg.title: Google Docs system architecture diagram
svg.desc: Full system architecture showing clients, API gateway, microservices, storage layers, and real-time collaboration infrastructure

g.text: Browser / mobile — OT engine, WebSocket client, local document cache
Auth (JWT), rate limiting, routing, TLS termination

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain the auth service in a Google Docs design')
sendPrompt('Explain the document CRUD service in a Google Docs design')
sendPrompt('How does the collaboration service handle OT operations?')
sendPrompt('How does RBAC work in a Google Docs system design?')
sendPrompt('What does the notification service do in this design?')

g.text: Op broadcast, presence events, change feed

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does WebSocket session management scale in Google Docs?')
g.text: Sticky sessions, op fan-out to connected clients

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does Redis store in a Google Docs design?')
sendPrompt('What does PostgreSQL store in a Google Docs design?')
sendPrompt('Why use Cassandra for the edit log in Google Docs?')
sendPrompt('Why use MongoDB for document snapshots in Google Docs?')
sendPrompt('What does S3 store in a Google Docs design?')

g.text: Snapshot compaction (every 50 ops) · CDN invalidation · Email/push dispatch
